selection: unambiguous single-subcategory clips, high/medium crosswalk confidence, >=30 clips per subcategory

policy.scale: MAD->sigma, widened 1.2x, and never tightened below the reasoned deviation
artifacts_excluded: envelope ADSR, sustain_ratio, decay time and crest factor calibrated from one-shot clips only: on multi-event clips they measure the uploader's arrangement, not the sound (attack tracks clip length at r=+0.72; a keypress reads 10 ms one-shot vs 3.0 s multi-event)
stationarity mass on the 0.0 clamp widens rather than tightens

gate_changes.detail: widening to the measured p01 (0.003935) reaches the feature's floor of 0 — it excludes nothing

not_calibrated.length_seconds: Clip duration in FSD50K is a Freesound upload artifact truncated to 0.3-30 s. It describes the uploader, not the sound.
not_calibrated.lufs: Freesound uploads carry arbitrary uploader-chosen normalization.
not_calibrated.root_mean_square_level: Same: absolute level is not a property of the source here.
not_calibrated.stereo_width: FSD50K is mono. The feature is undefined, not zero.
not_calibrated.sample_rate: A property of the dataset's encoding, not the sound.
not_calibrated.beats_per_minute: Not a target of this dataset.
not_calibrated.dc_offset: A recording defect, not a category property.
not_calibrated.clipping_density: A recording defect, and confounded by lossy-transcode overshoot.
not_calibrated.band_limit_high_hz: 57 % of FSD50K clips are band-limited below 17 kHz and 8 % sit in the 15–16.5 kHz MP3 lowpass band. Freesound uploads are routinely lossy-origin, transcoded to WAV for release. This feature would measure the uploader's codec, not the sound.
not_calibrated.voicing_ratio: The WebRTC VAD is telephony-tuned and fires on any harmonic energy. On this data it rates AMBIENCE-TRAFFIC 0.96 voiced and CROWDS-APPLAUSE 0.79 — those are facts about the detector, not the sounds. Calibrating to them would enshrine a broken feature. Blocked until a real voicing detector replaces the VAD.
